purpose: Schema reference for task files at tasks/{MM_DD_YY}/parallel_{name}.json -- the authoritative master record for each swarm with full task descriptions, context, critical instructions, file lists, dependencies, statuses, and completion summaries. Differentiates task files (rich, master-updated on every completion) from initialization.json (minimal, write-once, dashboard-driving). Note: filename retains 'xml' for historical reasons but task files are JSON.
gotchas: Filename says xml but the format is JSON -- legacy naming preserved to avoid breaking links
Task file status fields are updated on every worker completion (unlike initialization.json which is write-once)
Repair tasks are appended with id suffix 'r' to the relevant wave's tasks array
patterns: Mutable authoritative record vs write-once dashboard plan
Wave-grouped task arrays with descriptive wave names for human readability
conventions: Task IDs use 'wave.index' format and match agents[].id in initialization.json
Companion plan_{name}.md captures rationale; .json captures structure
